article_meta.title: Four days into war with Iran, at least one of the United States’ Gulf allies is already running low on crucial intercept

reasoning: The response includes a structural violation (markdown code fences) and multiple analysis errors: one clear attribution rule breach, several questionable/overstated bias flags, and missed author-prose bias instances. Only the first item is reasonably defensible.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged the quoted/scare-quoted term 'burning' that appears inside quotation marks and is tied to an unnamed source — an attribution violation. Other items were from author prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Three of four items are weak or incorrect: (2) misuses 'Omission of Source Attribution' despite explicit (albeit anonymous) attribution; (3) flags quoted language; (4) labels a neutral paraphrase as Negativity Bias without solid support.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed notable author-prose framing such as 'Now that the war is expanding, it’s a numbers game' (spin/word choice), 'The immediate concern though is...not the US' (slant/framing), and 'taking a toll on the arsenal' (loaded phrase).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Only the first item’s Sensationalism label is defensible; the second is the wrong category, and the third would be Word Choice only if it were the author’s term (it isn’t).
